threats.title: Weak Authentication Mechanism
threats.description: The system lacks detailed information about its authentication and authorization mechanisms. This could lead to attackers bypassing authentication entirely, or exploiting weak authentication schemes (e.g., simple password policies, lack of multi-factor authentication) to impersonate legitimate users.
threats.attack_vector: Exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication logic, brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, or social engineering to gain unauthorized access.
mitigations: Implement strong password policies and enforce regular password changes.
Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all users, especially administrators.
Utilize a robust authentication framework with proper session management and protection against common attacks like Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF).

threats.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability
threats.description: The system stores data in a relational database without proper input validation and sanitization. This creates a vulnerability to SQL injection attacks, allowing attackers to manipulate database queries, potentially leading to data breaches, data modification, or even complete system takeover.
threats.attack_vector: Injecting malicious SQL code into input fields (e.g., login forms, search boxes, data entry forms) to alter database queries.
mitigations: Implement parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ input validation and sanitization techniques to filter out malicious characters and code.
Regularly update database software to patch known vulnerabilities.

threats.title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) Vulnerability
threats.description: The system displays user-supplied data without proper encoding or sanitization. This creates a vulnerability to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks, allowing attackers to inject malicious JavaScript code into web pages viewed by other users, potentially stealing cookies, redirecting users to malicious websites, or defacing the website.
threats.attack_vector: Injecting malicious JavaScript code into input fields or URLs that are then executed by the web browser when the page is rendered.
mitigations: Implement output encoding and sanitization to prevent the execution of malicious JavaScript code.
Use a Content Security Policy (CSP) to restrict the sources from which the browser can load resources.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter out malicious requests.

threats.title: Insufficient Access Control
threats.description: Without clear information on access control mechanisms, it’s possible users could be granted elevated privileges unintentionally or through vulnerabilities. This could allow an attacker to gain unauthorized access to sensitive data or functionality.
threats.affected_component: Web Application, Database Server, System Services
threats.attack_vector: Exploiting vulnerabilities in the access control logic, misconfigured permissions, or privilege escalation flaws.
mitigations: Implement the principle of least privilege, granting users only the minimum necessary permissions.
Regularly review and audit user access rights.
Utilize a robust access control framework with proper authentication and authorization mechanisms.

threats.title: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
threats.description: The system is vulnerable to denial-of-service (DoS) attacks if it doesn't properly handle large or malicious inputs. An attacker could flood the system with requests or oversized data, exhausting server resources and rendering the application unavailable to legitimate users.
threats.attack_vector: Sending large amounts of data to the system, triggering computationally expensive operations, or exploiting vulnerabilities that cause excessive resource consumption.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting to restrict the number of requests from a single IP address.
Use input validation and sanitization to prevent the system from processing malicious data.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter out malicious requests.

gaps: Weak Authentication Mechanism - Insufficient focus on access control mechanisms
Insufficient Access Control - Limited coverage of CM controls
priorities: High - SQL Injection Vulnerability & Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) Vulnerability
Medium - Weak Authentication Mechanism & Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input

metadata.error: ReportRepository.create() got an unexpected keyword argument 'format'

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class web_app,auth_service,core_api,database threat

semgrep.config_file:
rules:
- id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Authentication Mechanism
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: SQL Injection Vulnerability
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) Vulnerability
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Insufficient Access Control
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-400
    owasp: A05:2021
    threat_category: Denial of Service
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
